human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for fintech-specific source quality, sponsor-bank and embedded-finance boundary handling, evidence traceability, refusal posture around legal and AML authority, and validated delivery completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to fintech and embedded-finance compliance work grounded in sponsor-bank programs, product and jurisdiction maps, complaint and dispute evidence, KYC and sanctions controls, third-party oversight, and payment-data obligations.

Authority sources cover interagency bank-fintech third-party guidance, joint fintech due-diligence guidance, CFPB compliance-management and Regulation E workflow guidance, FFIEC and FinCEN KYC sources, OFAC sanctions control guidance, and PCI DSS evidence standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope fintech execution, missing evidence, conflicting policy or regulator sources, legal-signoff refusal, policy-practice conflict, missing tenant facts, adjacent AML routing, regulator-facing escalation, overgeneralization risk, and tenant-specific adaptation handling.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal interpretation, AML and sanctions adjudication, regulator-facing response ownership, and formal certification requests.

notes: The package is suitable as a validated fintech and embedded-finance compliance specialist for spec-pack distribution and JIT materialization under orchestrator control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
